summary: Implement M2 agriculture, labor, population, grain, and cash scaffolding
acceptance_criteria: M2 WorldState scaffolding represents population groups, agricultural phases, available/committed labor, grain stock, cash stock, and minimal market/cash-flow state with branded IDs and explicit serializable schema fields.
All economic and population state mutations occur inside sim-core daily/monthly phases or through versioned GameCommand paths; UI/client stores cannot mutate authoritative state.
Player and AI actors use the same command type for M2 labor or mobilization decisions, with preview/apply validation and structured rejection reasons.
Mobilizing labor across farm timing demonstrably affects the next harvest in the 30 District/10 Settlement fixture.
Resource conservation and non-negative invariants cover population, labor assignment, grain, and cash; deterministic tie-breakers are specified for all ordered work.
No vassal politics, full war loop, formal historical character database, full diplomacy, runtime SQL/ORM, server, Rust/WASM, or new production dependency is introduced.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover M2 agriculture phase transitions, labor allocation, population/grain/cash conservation, and harvest impact from mistimed mobilization.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M2-ECON-POP-001
notes: This is scaffolding for Roadmap M2 only; do not build complete economy, taxation, war manpower, or balance systems.
Any save/protocol schema expansion must remain explicit and versioned and must include compatibility test evidence.
Lead-owned goal-mode, model-routing, and thread-registry updates are allowed only as orchestration recovery evidence for this task; they must not expand implementation scope.
